ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was recorded */,
  "co2" REAL /* CO2 level in parts per million (ppm) */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Relative humidity in percentage */,
  "light" REAL /* Light intensity in lux */,
  "pir" REAL /* Motion detection value (PIR) */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that recorded the data */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* ID of the building where the sensor is located */,
  "floor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the floor where the sensor is located */,
  "room_id" INTEGER /* ID of the room where the sensor is located */,
  "energy_consumption" REAL /* Energy consumption related to the sensor data */,
  "anomaly_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the data point is an anomaly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., CO2, humidity, light, PIR, temperature) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the sensor within the building */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "last_calibrated" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the sensor (active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "battery_level" REAL /* Battery level of the sensor (for wireless sensors) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., facility manager, maintenance, occupant) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
